_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to talk about how words and pictures are used to create characters, settings and events in stories by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander authors.

_source.w: We are learning to talk about how words and pictures are used to create characters, settings and events in stories by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander authors.
y: Understanding how language and images build a story helps us appreciate the craft of storytelling.
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander stories use language and images in rich, meaningful ways.
This helps us think more deeply about the stories we read and hear.
eg: We look at the illustrations in a book by a First Nations illustrator and discuss how they show the setting.
We find the words an Aboriginal author uses to describe a character and talk about what picture they paint.
We compare how two stories use language differently to describe their settings.
We draw a character based only on the words the author uses and compare our drawings.
We discuss how the pictures and words together tell us more than either one alone.
We retell a scene from a First Nations story, using the same describing words the author used.
